entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The implementation of covenants in Bitcoin, particularly through methods like CTV (CheckTemplateVerify), holds significant potential for enhancing the blockchain's functionality and expanding its use cases. Covenants could allow developers to create innovative projects that respond to real market demands. This technological advancement is not only pivotal for professional developers but also educational institutions, as it provides an opportunity for students to learn programming languages such as Sapio, diversifying their skills beyond more common languages like Solidity.
The introduction of covenants is expected to increase the demand for block space, moving beyond the typical uses by exchanges and privacy-focused applications like coinjoin. This shift could lead to more dynamic utilization of the Bitcoin blockchain, fostering a richer ecosystem of applications and services. Moreover, the financial aspect of Bitcoin development might see improvements; with broader applications and increased demand, funding for projects and developers could become less reliant on convincing a limited number of grant-makers.
Despite some public controversies and debates over the contentious nature of CTV, those involved in technical evaluations have not presented substantial technical objections (technical NACKs) against it. Discussions on mailing lists and IRC meetings have generally shown a consensus or at least an acceptance among developers regarding the adoption of CTV and other covenant proposals. This general agreement underscores the readiness of the community to adopt such changes without prolonged delays.
Regarding the process of implementing these covenants via a soft fork, there is a recommendation for streamlining the review processes for Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs). A preference was expressed for using BIP 8 or an enhanced version of it for implementing soft forks, although openness to other effective methods remains if they serve to improve the protocol. Implementing covenants before the next market cycle could provide a fertile ground for development during bear market periods, which is often when less speculative and more fundamentally focused innovation occurs. It is suggested that considering each case individually might be a more effective approach than waiting for broad consensus on future proposals, especially given the belief by some in the community that there is a narrowing window for making such changes before the protocol becomes too ossified for major updates.
